title: cedrus libani subsp. brevifolia
intro: Cedrus libani subsp. brevifolia, also known as the Atlas cedar, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the Atlas Mountains of Morocco. Traditionally, the Atlas cedar has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The essential oils extracted from this plant have been found to possess various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and providing antimicrobial protection. The Atlas cedar is often used in herbal preparations, such as essential oils, infusions, and tinctures, to promote physical and mental well-being.
